message.content:
You are an autonomous engineering agent working in the repo at C:\repos\oracle (Windows, PowerShell primary; Bash tool available for POSIX). Your single job: implement the PROPER, permanent, root-cause fixes for four model-adapter bugs and carry the work all the way through to a proven, deployed, production-verified end state — WITHOUT asking the repo owner anything. The owner's standing mandate: "Proper, long-term fix only. Never a band-aid. Never accept anything less than perfect — not for now, not ever." Honor that as a floor.

YOUR FULL, AUTHORITATIVE SPEC IS THE FILE: C:\repos\oracle\fix_ADAPTER_BUGS.md
Read it completely and in full FIRST, then also read C:\repos\oracle\fix_TESTPLAN.md (the bake-off protocol), C:\repos\oracle\fix_TESTPLAN_RESULTS.md (the prior blocked run), C:\repos\oracle\AGENTS.md and C:\repos\oracle\HANDOFF.md (operating guide + state), and C:\repos\oracle\CLAUDE.md plus the user CLAUDE.md rules. Everything you need is in those files or discoverable in the repo. Do not ask me to clarify anything — make the most correct decision and proceed. If a credential for a specific model is genuinely absent from the environment, do NOT ask for it and do NOT skip silently: verify everything else and report that one model as "unverified — key absent" in your final report. Never request a secret in chat; use keys already set in the environment.

The four bugs (full detail + acceptance criteria in fix_ADAPTER_BUGS.md):
1. OpenAI strict structured output rejects the extraction schema (optional fields missing from `required`). Partial fix exists (`stripIncompatibleFields` in openai-adapter.ts); make it perfect (nullable optionals, OpenAI-only, Zod still accepts null).
2. Gemini Google-API hard 60s timeout. Partial fix exists (180s + configurable). Verify both auth paths, no remaining hard-coded 60s anywhere.
3. Gemini `thinkingLevel` breaks gemini-2.5-flash vision — STILL BROKEN: catalog marks gemini-2.5-flash supportsReasoningControls:true, so the current guard does nothing. Find the true root cause against the LIVE API (thinkingLevel enum vs thinkingBudget, vision+thinking incompatibility, or wrong capability granularity) and fix it via the catalog/capability layer — NO hard-coded model names in adapter logic.
4. Settings value double-JSON-encoded. Data already repaired in prod; find and fix the WRITER (model-picker → API route → settings table), make writes idempotent, add a regression guard.

Then, in order:
- PROVE each fix against the LIVE provider APIs (OpenAI key set; Google Gemini via key or service-account — test the path prod uses; Qwen3-vl via DashScope INTL region, DASHSCOPE_API_KEY set + DASHSCOPE_BASE_URL=intl; Vertex via ADC as known-good). Use/extend packages/ai/src/__verify__.
- Run gates: pnpm -w typecheck, lint, build, and any ai tests/verify smokes. Record a baseline first; introduce no new failures; never @ts-expect-error or .catch() past real failures.
- DEPLOY the Trigger.dev worker (project proj_wgpzsvhmsopqhvwqaycn) to the env prod ingestion uses; confirm the new worker version is live. For ALL async runs, BLOCK on completion with mcp__trigger__wait_for_run_to_complete — never single-poll and move on.
- RE-RUN the full bake-off per fix_TESTPLAN.md now that the candidates execute: extraction = gpt-4o-mini, gemini-2.5-flash (Google), gpt-4.1-mini, gemini-3.1-flash-lite, and the Vertex gemini-2.5-flash route (likely winner — extracted 56-81 claims earlier); vision = qwen3-vl vs gemini-2.5-flash (post-fix). Isolate each model into a one-element pool so pool-advancement can't mask failures. Capture claims/valid-rejected/cost/latency/quality (judge faithfulness to the diagram's known ground truths: Gina→"Review Audit and send to factory"/white column, Carlos→"SKUs creation"/peach column). Keep heavy dumps in files, not inline.
- SETTINGS SAFETY: record current prod settings verbatim before mutating; never write a double-encoded value (read back to confirm after every write); if anything fails, restore the known-good safe pair extraction=gemini-3.1-flash-lite, vision=qwen3-vl — never leave prod on a broken model.
- SET PRODUCTION to the genuine winner (quality first — the owner rejects under-extraction like lite's 7-claims-from-74-edges — then cost/latency). Keep enforce_model_capabilities=true; order pools so only WORKING models form the fallback chain.
- COMMIT (git add specific paths only, never -A) with conventional-commit message(s) + a `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer, and PUSH to origin/main (the deployed worker must match committed source). Update HANDOFF.md (and AGENTS.md/docs/configuration.md only if a documented fact changed, e.g. new env var GOOGLE_GEMINI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS or the chosen prod models). Never commit secrets, .env.local, keys, or scratch dumps.
- DEFINITION OF DONE: production runs a real image ingestion end-to-end (vision→extraction→claims) on the DEPLOYED worker with the chosen models and yields rich, schema-valid, faithful claims. Do not finish before that passes.

Return ONE comprehensive final report (this is the message I receive): per-bug root cause + fix file:line + live-API proof; the full bake-off results table with the named winner; the exact prod end-state (routes, pools, enforce flag) read back and confirmed single-encoded; the live worker deploy id; the commit SHA(s) pushed and files in each; and anything you could not verify, stated plainly. Be thorough and exact.

- designflow-e2e-tester: Hybrid AI end-to-end tester for the DesignFlow app (designflow-frontend Angular UI + designflow-bff / designflow-backend / -tracking / -item-master / -data-syncing Node APIs). Drives the real app in a browser like a human would — logging in, clicking through every screen, filling forms with valid/invalid/edge-case data, hunting for broken behavior — AND writes durable Playwright regression tests into the existing e2e/ suites. Use this whenever the user wants to test changes, "test the app like a human", do exploratory/QA testing, find bugs in a flow, verify a feature end-to-end, smoke-test before a deploy, or grow E2E coverage. Trigger even when they don't say "Playwright" or "E2E" — phrases like "make sure my changes didn't break anything", "test all the screens", "click through the app", or "QA this" all apply.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# fix_ADAPTER_BUGS.md — Permanent, root-cause fixes for the four model-adapter bugs
2	
3	**Audience:** a fresh AI engineering session with no memory of how these bugs were found.
4	**Mandate from the repo owner (Albert):** *"Proper, long-term fix. Never a short-term fix or band-aid. Never accept anything less than perfect, not for now, not ever."* Treat every instruction here as a floor, not a ceiling. If you find a deeper or more correct fix than what is written, take it — but never a narrower or more temporary one.
5	
6	You must carry this all the way to completion **without asking the owner anything**: fix → prove against live provider APIs → deploy the worker → re-run the full extraction/vision bake-off → set production to the genuinely-best models → commit + push → write the results report. Everything you need is below or discoverable in the repo. Do not stop at "code compiles." Done means "proven correct against the real APIs in production and the bake-off re-run."
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 0. Context: what these bugs are and how they were discovered
11	
12	The Oracle ingests images and documents in two passes:
13	1. **VISION** — a vision model transcribes an uploaded image to text (`default_vision_route`).
14	2. **EXTRACTION** — a text model turns that transcript into claims JSON (`default_extraction_route`).
15	
16	Inference path: `OracleAIClient → ModelRouter → provider adapter`. Approved models live in DB settings pools (`model_pool_extraction`, etc.). When a model fails, the pool advances to the next candidate (`AllCandidatesFailedError` only if *all* fail). **This pool-advancement masked the bugs:** a model that 400s instantly looks "fine" because the chain silently moves on to one that works.
17	
18	A controlled bake-off (isolating each model into a one-element pool) revealed that **3 of 4 extraction models and 1 of 2 vision models are hard-broken in current production.** The only models that actually run today are extraction=`gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (works but under-extracts — ~7 claims from a ~74-edge diagram) and vision=`qwen3-vl` (verified accurate). Everything else fails before producing a claim. The four root causes are below.
19	
20	The owner does **not** want the weak-but-working `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` as the permanent extractor. He wants every candidate model genuinely working so the bake-off can pick the best on quality, not on "it was the only one that didn't crash."
21	
22	**Partial fixes already exist in the working tree** (started by a prior session). Do not assume they are correct or complete — verify each against the acceptance criteria and the live API. Some are wrong (see Bug 3).
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 1. THE FOUR BUGS
27	
28	### Bug 1 — OpenAI strict structured output rejects the extraction schema
29	**Symptom:** `gpt-4o-mini` and `gpt-4.1-mini` extraction calls 400 instantly with an error about a missing property (e.g. `Missing 'sensitivityReason'`). Both OpenAI extraction models are therefore unusable.
30	
31	**Root cause:** OpenAI's strict JSON-schema mode (`response_format.json_schema.strict: true`) requires that **every** property appear in the object's `required` array (and `additionalProperties: false`). The Zod→JSON-schema converter only lists non-`.optional()` keys in `required`. The extraction schema has several optional fields (`packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts` — `.optional()` at roughly lines 152, 190, 201, 205, 210, 223, including `sensitivityReason` / `sensitivityFlags`). Those optional keys are absent from `required`, so strict mode 400s.
32	
33	**Current partial fix (verify, then make perfect):** `packages/ai/src/providers/openai-adapter.ts` has `stripIncompatibleFields()` → `walk()` (~line 387) that deletes `$schema`/`default` and recomputes `required = Object.keys(properties)` for every object node. It is applied in both `generateObject` (line ~110) and `submitBatch` (line ~174).
34	
35	**Why this is not yet "perfect" and what you must do:**
36	- Forcing a genuinely-optional field into `required` without also making it **nullable** forces the model to always emit a value, which is semantically wrong for fields that are meant to be absent sometimes. The canonical OpenAI-strict pattern is: *every property is in `required`, and formerly-optional properties become nullable* (`type: ["string","null"]`, or a union with `{"type":"null"}`). Implement that in `walk()`: for each property key that was **not** originally in the object's `required` set, union its type with `null` (handle `type` as string, `type` as array, and `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`$ref` shapes). Then recompute `required` to all keys.
37	- Confirm the downstream Zod parse still accepts a `null` for those fields (the schema uses `.optional()`; `null` is not `undefined`). If Zod rejects `null`, either map `.optional()` → `.nullish()` in the schema, or normalize `null`→`undefined` before validation in the OpenAI adapter. Pick whichever keeps a single source of truth and does not weaken validation for the other providers. **Do not** silently coerce or `.catch()` away validation errors (a prior session tried that; it was reverted as a band-aid).
38	- The strip/nullable transform must be **OpenAI-only**. Gemini/Vertex must keep receiving the original schema (they do not require all-keys-required and they reject some OpenAI-isms). Verify you are not changing the shared `zodToJsonSchema` output for the other adapters.
39	
40	**Acceptance:** `gpt-4o-mini` AND `gpt-4.1-mini` complete a real extraction call against the live OpenAI API on the bake-off document and return schema-valid claims (no 400, no refusal). Optional fields that the model legitimately omits come back as `null`/absent and pass Zod.
41	
42	---
43	
44	### Bug 2 — Gemini (Google Developer API) hard 60-second timeout
45	**Symptom:** `google/gemini-2.5-flash` extraction aborts at ~60.1s on dense image-derived transcripts, every time.
46	
47	**Root cause:** a hard-coded `AbortSignal.timeout(60_000)` in the Google Gemini adapter.
48	
49	**Current partial fix (verify):** `packages/ai/src/providers/google-gemini-adapter.ts` now defines `DEFAULT_GEMINI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 180_000`, a `requestTimeoutMs` constructor option, and a `GOOGLE_GEMINI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` env override; the SDK client gets `httpOptions: { timeout: this.requestTimeoutMs }` and the OAuth/REST path uses `AbortSignal.timeout(this.requestTimeoutMs)`.
50	
51	**What you must do:** Verify there is **no remaining hard-coded 60s** anywhere in this adapter or any other adapter (grep `60_000`, `60000`, `timeout`). Confirm the timeout applies on **both** code paths (API-key SDK path and service-account OAuth/REST path). Confirm 180s is comfortably above the real p99 for a dense extraction (the bake-off transcripts can take >60s; if you observe calls approaching 180s, raise the default and note it). A timeout must never be the thing that kills a legitimate call.
52	
53	**Acceptance:** `google/gemini-2.5-flash` completes a real extraction on the bake-off document via the Google Developer API without an abort, on whichever auth path production actually uses.
54	
55	---
56	
57	### Bug 3 — Gemini `thinkingLevel` unconditionally attached, breaks gemini-2.5-flash vision (NOT actually fixed yet)
58	**Symptom:** `gemini-2.5-flash` as a **vision** model 400s with "Thinking level not supported" (or equivalent).
59	
60	**Root cause:** `geminiThinkingConfig()` attached a `thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel` to every call.
61	
62	**Current partial fix is INSUFFICIENT — this is the one that is still broken.** `geminiThinkingConfig(route)` now early-returns `{}` when `!route.supportsReasoningControls`. **But the model catalog marks `gemini-2.5-flash` with `supportsReasoningControls: true`** (`packages/ai/src/routes/catalog.ts:49`). So for `gemini-2.5-flash` the guard does nothing — `thinkingLevel` is still attached and vision still 400s. The partial fix only helps models already flagged `false`.
63	
64	**You must determine the true root cause against the live API and fix it properly.** Investigate and decide among (not limited to) these — verify empirically, do not guess:
65	- Whether `gemini-2.5-flash` rejects the **`thinkingLevel` enum** specifically but accepts a numeric **`thinkingBudget`** (the older field). If so, the adapter should emit the field shape the target model actually supports — i.e. capability must encode *how* a model expresses thinking, not just a boolean.
66	- Whether thinking config is simply invalid when the request includes **image input** (vision) for this model, regardless of text support — in which case vision calls must never carry thinking config.
67	- Whether the catalog flag for `gemini-2.5-flash` is itself wrong for the vision use, requiring a more precise capability model (e.g. `thinking` supported for text but the `thinkingLevel` enum unsupported on this model generation).
68	
69	The "perfect" fix is a capability model that is correct per-model and per-modality, driven from `catalog.ts`/`candidates.ts` metadata — **not** a special-case `if (modelId === 'gemini-2.5-flash')`. No hard-coded model names in adapter logic (the owner has been explicit and repeatedly insistent: **nothing model-specific may be hard-coded in adapters**; model facts live in the catalog/capability layer and flow through the route).
70	
71	**Acceptance:** `gemini-2.5-flash` completes a real **vision** transcription of the bake-off image via the live API with no thinking-related 400, while any model that *does* support `thinkingLevel` still receives it for reasoning-bearing roles. Re-verify Bug 2's `google/gemini-2.5-flash` extraction still passes after this change.
72	
73	---
74	
75	### Bug 4 — Settings value double-JSON-encoded (data fixed; find & fix the WRITER)
76	**Symptom:** `default_extraction_reasoning_effort` was stored as a double-encoded string (`"\"off\""` / `"\"google/gemini-2.5-flash\""`-style) in the `settings` table, so consumers read a quoted string instead of the value. The **data** has already been repaired to a single-encoded `"off"` in production. **The code path that produced it has NOT been fixed.**
77	
78	**Root cause to find:** trace the write path from `apps/web/app/admin/settings/_components/model-picker.tsx` (it `fetch`/POSTs a JSON body at ~lines 267, 280) → the API route handler it calls → the `settings` table write (Drizzle `settings` schema, imported in `apps/web/app/admin/settings/page.tsx`). Somewhere a value that is already a JSON string is `JSON.stringify`'d again (double-encode), or a value that should be stored raw is being stringified when the column/consumer expects the bare value. Find the exact line and fix it so a write is **idempotent**: writing `off` stores `off` (or a single, consistent canonical encoding), and reading it back yields `off`. Whatever canonical form you choose (raw text vs. single-JSON), make the **writer, the reader, and every other settings consumer agree** — verify there is not a second writer (e.g. the bake-off `_set` tooling, server actions, or migrations) using a different convention.
79	
80	**Acceptance:** Round-trip test: set each model/effort setting through the **real admin write path**, read it back through the **real consumer path** (`page.tsx` loader + the router's settings reader), and confirm no extra quoting at any layer. Add a regression guard (unit test or a `__verify__` assertion) that fails if a settings value is double-encoded.
81	
82	---
83	
84	## 2. PROVE IT — no fix counts until it runs against the live APIs
85	
86	For each bug, write or reuse a small Node/tsx harness under `packages/ai/src/__verify__/` (there is already `r-providers-smoke.ts` you can model on) **or** a throwaway script you delete afterward, that:
87	- Builds the real route for the model from the catalog.
88	- Calls the real adapter `generateObject` (extraction) / `generateText`/vision (transcription) against the **live provider API** using the env keys already configured in this workspace.
89	- Asserts a schema-valid, non-empty result.
90	
91	Provider/region notes you will need:
92	- **OpenAI** (`gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4.1-mini`): `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set.
93	- **Google Gemini** (`google/gemini-2.5-flash`): Developer API via `GEMINI_API_KEY`/`GOOGLE_API_KEY`, or service-account OAuth via `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON`. Determine which production uses and test that path.
94	- **Vertex Gemini** (`vertex/...` routes): ADC/service-account; separate adapter from Google. (This adapter already works — it produced 56–81 claims on the bake-off doc earlier. Keep it as a known-good comparison point.)
95	- **Qwen vision** (`qwen3-vl`): DashScope, **international region**. `DASHSCOPE_API_KEY` is set and `DASHSCOPE_BASE_URL` must point at `dashscope-intl` (the model + key live there, not the US default). Already verified working — use as the vision known-good.
96	
97	If a provider key is genuinely missing for a model you must test, do **not** ask the owner and do **not** skip it silently: report it explicitly in the final results as "could not verify — key X absent," and verify everything else. (Per the workspace credential rule, you may *use* keys already set in the environment, but you must never ask the owner to hand you a secret in chat.)
98	
99	---
100	
101	## 3. GATES — run before deploying
102	
103	From repo root, in order; everything must pass (or be no-worse than the pre-existing baseline you record first):
104	```
105	pnpm -w typecheck      # or: pnpm turbo typecheck
106	pnpm -w lint
107	pnpm -w build
108	pnpm --filter @oracle/ai test   # if a test script exists; plus any __verify__ smokes you added
109	```
110	Record the baseline (run them once before you change anything) so you can prove you introduced no new failures. Do not weaken or `// @ts-expect-error` your way past a real failure.
111	
112	---
113	
114	## 4. DEPLOY THE WORKER
115

10	## 0. Orientation (you have no prior context — read this)
11	
12	**The Oracle** is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph (`pnpm`+`turbo` TypeScript monorepo). Employees upload documents/images; background jobs turn them into **claims** (operational facts) each backed by a verbatim **quote**.
13	
14	A flowchart **image** is processed in **two passes**:
15	- **Pass 1 — VISION:** a vision model transcribes the image to faithful TEXT (persisted as `document_chunks`). Controlled by setting `default_vision_route`.
16	- **Pass 2 — EXTRACTION:** an extraction model reads that text and emits structured **claims** (JSON validated against a strict Zod schema). Controlled by setting `default_extraction_route`.
17	
18	Repo layout you need: `apps/workers/` = Trigger.dev background jobs (incl. `document-ingestion`); `packages/ai/` = the model router + provider adapters; `packages/db/` = Postgres schema (Supabase). Deploys: workers via Trigger.dev (manual), web via Vercel (push to `main`).
19	
20	**What is already confirmed working (as of 2026-06-26, do NOT re-litigate):**
21	- Vision on `qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking` works and produces a rich transcription (~11–13k chars, ~84 one-line edges) with **no silent fallback**.
22	- The model-routing refactor is live: no `fallbackRouteId`, failures fail loud, the approved **pool** is the fallback chain, `enforce_model_capabilities=true`, and per-attempt records land in `model_run_attempts`.
23	- The extraction BLOCKER (loose-JSON Qwen → 0 claims) is resolved by using a native-strict-JSON model.
24	
25	**The open question this plan answers:** *which* extraction model, and *which* vision model, give the best quality for the money. The current pick `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` is strict-JSON and unblocks extraction but **under-extracts badly** — it returned only **7 claims (1 dependency)** from a transcription containing **84 handoff edges**. We need a real comparison.

It has ~63 boxes across 14 swimlanes (columns = roles/departments: Albert, Buyer, Sales, Creative direction, Junior designer, Technical designers, Creative designers, Sourcing, Production, Carlos, Gina, Licensing Team, Licensor, Factories) and ~80+ directional arrows (handoffs, with conditional branch labels like "If Audit: Fail"). 69	## 3. Core mechanics you MUST understand before running
70	
71	### 3.1 How to swap the model (no redeploy needed — settings are read at runtime)
72	Two settings rows in the `settings` table drive the passes:
73	- `default_extraction_route` — the extraction model (e.g. `"openai/gpt-4o-mini"`).
74	- `default_vision_route` — the vision model.
75	
76	### 3.2 Capability enforcement is ON — the model must be in the approved POOL and capability-valid
77	`enforce_model_capabilities=true`. The resolver only uses a model for a stage if it is (a) the configured route or a member of that stage's **approved pool**, AND (b) capability-valid. So **before** selecting a model you MUST add it to the pool:
78	- Extraction pool: `model_pool_extraction` (currently `["google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite","qwen/qwen3.7-plus","qwen/qwen3.7-max"]`).
79	- Vision pool: `model_pool_vision` (may or may not be enforced for aux — verify; if vision is single-pick, just set `default_vision_route`).
80	
81	**CONFIRM the exact catalog model ids first.** The picker stores `provider/modelId`. Do NOT guess. Get the real ids from the admin model picker (`/admin/settings`) OR query the catalog:
82	```sql
83	select model_id, provider, supports_structured_output, supports_vision, context_window_tokens,
84	       input_price_per_million, output_price_per_million
85	from model_capabilities
86	where model_id ilike '%gpt-4o-mini%' or model_id ilike '%gpt-4.1-mini%'
87	   or model_id ilike '%gemini-2.5-flash%'
88	order by model_id;
89	```
90	Use the EXACT `provider/model_id` the catalog returns. If a target model is not in `model_capabilities`, it may not be selectable under capability enforcement — note that as a finding and pick the closest catalog id.
91	
92	### 3.3 The run sequence for ONE configuration (memorize — you repeat it per model)
93	For a given `(vision model, extraction model)` config:
94	```bash
95	# 1. Set the pool + the selection (example SQL — adapt model ids):
96	#    UPDATE settings SET value = '["openai/gpt-4o-mini","google/gemini-2.5-flash","openai/gpt-4.1-mini"]'::jsonb WHERE key='model_pool_extraction';
97	#    UPDATE settings SET value = '"openai/gpt-4o-mini"'::jsonb WHERE key='default_extraction_route';
98	#    (and default_vision_route as needed)
99	
100	# 2. Clean + reset the test document, then trigger ingestion:
101	PROD_URL="<oracle_session_pooler>" DOCUMENT_ID="9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a" APPLY=1 \
102	  node scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs
103	#    then trigger the 'document-ingestion' task with payload {"documentId":"9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a"}
104	#    (Trigger MCP trigger_task, env=prod, or the dashboard). Wait for the run to COMPLETE.
105	```
106	`reevaluate-document.mjs` deletes the doc's prior claims/chunks/candidates and resets it to `pending_processing`; it aborts safely if any claim is in the Brain/a contradiction/a gap. The trigger then re-runs BOTH passes.
107	
108	### 3.4 CRITICAL fairness caveat for the EXTRACTION bake-off
109	Each full reprocess re-runs vision (Pass 1), and vision is mildly non-deterministic. To compare EXTRACTION models fairly, you MUST **hold vision constant** (`default_vision_route = qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking` for all three extraction runs) AND **capture each run's transcription stats** (char count + edge count, query in §A). Before comparing extraction outputs, confirm the three transcriptions are comparable (within ~15% on chars and edge count). If one run's transcription is materially different, RE-RUN that extraction config until the input is comparable, or explicitly flag the input difference in your analysis. Do not compare extraction quality across materially different inputs.

1	# AGENTS.md — The Oracle Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this first. It is the canonical operating guide for developers and AI coding sessions working in `C:\repos\oracle`.
4	
5	## 1. Project summary
6	
7	The Oracle is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph for POP Creations / Spruce Line. Employees interact with it through chat and document uploads; workers extract operational claims with quote-level evidence; deterministic validators gate promotion into approved claims; synthesis workers maintain traceable Brain sections; admin screens review runs, caches, claims, gaps, contradictions, and taxonomy proposals. The outcome that matters is explainable business knowledge: every important answer or synthesis artifact must be traceable back to messages, document chunks, or approved claims.
8	
9	## 2. Multi-model AI note
10	
11	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
12	
13	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
14	
15	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
16	
17	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
18	
19	Always start with:
20	
21	- `AGENTS.md`
22	
23	Then load additional docs only when relevant — do not bulk-read every `.md` file.
24	
25	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
26	|---|---|---|
27	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
28	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md` if present, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
29	| Add or change AI provider adapter or model catalog | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` (adapter table + data flow), provider files under `packages/ai/src/providers/`, `DECISIONS.md` | Worker or webhook code |
30	| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets | `AGENTS.md` §11, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
31	| Pull secrets from 1Password via the MCP server or `op` CLI | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/1password.md` | Unrelated architecture docs |
32	| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
33	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md` §12, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
34	| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
35	| Add or change a worker task | `AGENTS.md` §6 task-to-file, `apps/workers/src/trigger/`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow changes | Front-end app code unless there is a matching UI/API hook |
36	| Change Teams transcript ingestion (Graph path) | `AGENTS.md` §6 + §10 quirks, `docs/architecture.md` §"Teams transcript ingestion" | Recall docs unless both paths are affected |
37	| Change Recall.ai live bot path | `AGENTS.md` §6 + §10 quirks, `docs/architecture.md` §"Teams live participation (Recall.ai)" | Microsoft Graph Teams ingestion docs |
38	| Investigate a bug or incident | `AGENTS.md` §13 (critical incidents), docs for the affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
39	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
40	| Implement the no-fallback / model-capability / message-batching refactor | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and the matching spec: `fix_remove_fallbacks.md` (remove silent fallbacks + hard-coded models), `fix_resolve_ts.md` (runtime capability enforcement), `fix_claim_extr.md` (conversation-aware batching) | the other two `fix_*.md` unless the work overlaps |
41	| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
42	| Change the remote MCP knowledge endpoint (tools agents query) | `AGENTS.md` §10 MCP quirk, `apps/web/lib/mcp/README.md`, `apps/web/lib/mcp/*`, `apps/web/app/api/mcp/[transport]/route.ts` | Unrelated chat/worker code |
43	| China bilingual claim layer / claim translation / asking China-team members to verify a claim | `AGENTS.md` §7–§10, `china_imp.md` (design + resolved decisions), `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts`, `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-translation.ts`, `apps/web/app/admin/claims/*` (verification reuses main's `claim_review_question` + review-groups) | Teams/Recall docs; provider-adapter internals |
44	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
45	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
46	| Product/spec contract or AI-retrofit provenance | `AGENTS.md`, `oracle_master_spec.md`, relevant `docs/oracle/*` file, `oracle_ai_architecture_prompt caching.md` only if prompt-cache retrofit history is directly relevant | Current deployment/config docs unless operations are affected |
47	
48	Rules:
49	- MUST be task-based.
50	- MUST NOT become a flat list of every Markdown file.
51	- Read `HANDOFF.md` whenever it exists — it captures what is in-progress or unfinished.
52	- Update this documentation map when documentation files are added, removed, renamed, or repurposed.
53	- `docs/oracle/` — deeper AI-retrofit reference material; only read when the task touches the AI-retrofit spec directly.
54	- `oracle_master_spec.md` and `oracle_ai_architecture_prompt caching.md` are historical/spec reference files, not default orientation docs.
55	
56	## Five-minute orientation
57	
58	If you are new to this repo, read only this path first:
59	
60	1. `README.md` for the repo shape.
61	2. This file through §9 for operating rules, what to touch, external IDs, services, and ignore rules.
62	3. `HANDOFF.md` if it exists.
63	4. The single topic doc named by the table above.
64	
65	Do not open every Markdown file. Most tasks need `AGENTS.md` plus one topic doc and the affected source files.
66	
67	## 3. Repository structure
68	
69	This is a `pnpm` + `turbo` TypeScript monorepo.
70	
71	Code we own:
72	
73	- `apps/web/` — Next.js 16 App Router app, API routes, admin UI, employee chat UI
74	- `apps/workers/` — Trigger.dev tasks and worker config
75	- `packages/ai/` — `OracleAIClient`, retrieval, prompts, provider adapters, model catalog
76	- `packages/auth/` — Supabase auth helpers and employee identity linker
77	- `packages/db/` — Drizzle schema, client, migrate/seed scripts
78	- `packages/oracle-engines/` — deterministic extraction and synthesis logic
79	- `packages/shared/` — shared types/constants
80	- `docs/` — project documentation
81	- root markdown files — `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `china_imp.md`, and the planning docs `fix_remove_fallbacks.md` / `fix_resolve_ts.md` / `fix_claim_extr.md` / `fix_TESTPLAN.md` (self-contained specs/session plans — load only when doing that specific work)
82	
83	Generated code:
84	
85	- `packages/db/migrations/0*.sql` — Drizzle-generated SQL
86	- `packages/db/migrations/meta/` — Drizzle metadata
87	
88	Third-party / framework code:
89	
90	- `node_modules/`
91	- `apps/web/components/ui/` — shadcn-generated primitives; treat as framework layer
92	
93	Build artifacts and caches:
94	
95	- `apps/web/.next/`
96	- `dist/`
97	- `.turbo/`
98	- `.vercel/`
99	- `.cache/`
100	- `coverage/`
101	- `packages/ai/evals/runs/`
102	
103	Docs:
104	
105	- `docs/architecture.md`
106	- `docs/development.md`
107	- `docs/configuration.md`
108	- `docs/deployment.md`
109	- `docs/wet-test-walkthrough.md`
110	- `docs/oracle/` — deeper AI-retrofit reference material
111	- `oracle_master_spec.md` — product/spec contract
112	- `oracle_ai_architecture_prompt caching.md` — historical AI architecture/prompt-cache reference
113	- `china_imp.md` — design + resolved decisions for the China bilingual claim layer (Phase 1 implemented; the code is the source of truth, this doc captures rationale). Read only for China bilingual / translation / recertification work.
114	
115	Scripts:
116	
117	- `scripts/verify-catalog.ts` — provider/model catalog inspection
118	- `scripts/refresh-catalog.ts` — model catalog refresh against the real DB
119	- `packages/db/src/{migrate,seed,verify-identities,inspect-auth-users}.ts`
120	- `scripts/test-teams-transcript-access.ps1` — no-dep PowerShell probe: does the tenant grant the app Teams transcript access?
121	- `scripts/diagnose-transcripts.ps1` — tries `getAllTranscripts` variants for an organizer (scheduled meetings only)
122	- `scripts/create-adhoc-subscription.ps1` — one-off `adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` subscription creation (webhook must be live first; the `teams-subscription-manager` worker is the production path)
123	- `scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` — guarded single-document re-evaluation: deletes a document's prior claims/chunks/candidates/batches/tags and resets it to `pending_processing` (DRY-RUN by default; `APPLY=1` to act; aborts if any target claim is in the Brain/a contradiction/a gap/multi-source). Run with the prod session-pooler URL: `PROD_URL=<pooler> DOCUMENT_ID=<id> APPLY=1 node scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs`, then trigger `document-ingestion`.
124	
125	Migrations:

127	- `packages/db/src/schema.ts` — source of truth
128	- `packages/db/migrations/0*.sql` — generated DDL
129	- `packages/db/migrations/sql/*.sql` — hand-written constraints, views, data migrations, seeds

134	- `.github/workflows/pr-check.yml` — current CI gate

288	## 10. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
289	
290	### One employee can have many auth identities
291	
292	Looks like:
293	`employees` still has deprecated auth columns, so `employee_identities` looks redundant.
294	
295	Actually:
296	`employee_identities` is the real auth-link table. The deprecated columns remain only as transition leftovers.
297	
298	Why:
299	One person may log in through Google and Microsoft 365 and still be the same employee.
300	
301	Do not change because:
302	Collapsing back to one auth identity per employee breaks real user linkage and RLS helpers.
303	
304	### Employee removal is soft-disable, not hard delete
305	
306	What changed:
307	Admin -> Employees exposes Disable/Re-enable controls backed by `employees.disabled_at`.
308	
309	Why:
310	Employee rows are referenced by identities, messages, claims/evidence, documents, assignments, review events, and audit history. Hard-deleting a person can break provenance and historical records, while `disabled_at` already blocks login/linking and active RLS helpers.
311	
312	Future sessions should:
313	Use `apps/web/app/admin/employees/_components/employee-access-form.tsx` and `updateEmployeeAccess()` for GUI access changes. Do not add hard-delete employee buttons unless you first design archival/reference cleanup across all employee FKs.
314	
315	### All inference must go through `OracleAIClient`
316	
317	Looks like:
318	It would be simpler for routes or workers to call provider SDKs directly.
319	
320	Actually:
321	The provider adapters are the only supported inference boundary. They own prompt shaping, provider-native caching, reasoning translation, and usage normalization.
322	
323	Why:
324	The project depends on provider-specific caching and structured-output behavior that generic wrappers and ad hoc calls hide.
325	
326	Do not change because:
327	Direct SDK calls bypass context-pack logging, cache observability, route fallback, and consistent validation.
328	
329	### Log the actual AI result route, not only the planned route
330	
331	Looks like:
332	The caller already resolved a route before calling `OracleAIClient`, so it can log that route in `model_runs` and `model_run_usage_details`.
333	
334	Actually:
335	`ModelRouter` may dispatch to a non-primary approved candidate from the model pool. `OracleTextResult` and `OracleObjectResult` carry `routeId`, `provider`, `modelId`, `attemptedRoutes`, and `usedNonPrimary` after dispatch.
336	
337	Why:
338	Cost/cache dashboards and model-routing debugging need the route that actually ran, while `model_run_attempts` preserves each candidate tried.
339	
340	Do not change because:
341	Logging the pre-dispatch route hides non-primary dispatch and makes cache-hit/cost accounting wrong. New AI callers should use the result metadata when writing usage rows and call `logModelRunAttempts` / `logAllCandidatesFailedAttempts` for candidate visibility.
342	
343	### Chat retrieval is deterministic, not AI-SDK tool calling
344	
345	Looks like:
346	The chat route used to define `search_company_knowledge` and `check_open_gaps` tools, so re-adding `tools` to `providerOptions` might seem like restoring agentic retrieval.
347	
348	Actually:
349	The native provider adapters do not execute Vercel AI SDK tool definitions from `providerOptions`. The chat route now performs retrieval deterministically before the model call and injects recent messages, open gaps, approved claims, and Brain snippets into prompt blocks.
350	
351	Why:
352	All inference goes through raw provider adapters to preserve provider-native cache/usage fields. Passing AI SDK tools through this boundary looked useful but was decorative unless every native adapter learned tool orchestration.
353	
354	Do not change because:
355	Reintroducing AI SDK tools in the chat route can create a false sense that the model is searching live. Add retrieval through `searchWithRetrievalPlan()` and prompt blocks, or implement tool orchestration explicitly inside the native adapter boundary.
356	
357	### OpenRouter is enrichment-only
358	
359	Looks like:
360	`openrouter.ts` and OpenRouter-related code mean inference still runs through OpenRouter.
361	
362	Actually:
363	OpenRouter is only used to enrich the admin-side model catalog with pricing/capability metadata.
364	
365	Why:
366	Inference requires native provider features and exact usage fields; catalog enrichment does not.
367	
368	Do not change because:
369	Reintroducing OpenRouter into the inference path would erase provider-native cache and usage behavior that the rest of the system expects.
370	
371	### `google/*` model settings are real Gemini API routes, not Vertex aliases
372	
373	What changed:
374	`google/*` model IDs now resolve to the `GoogleGeminiAdapter`, while curated `vertex_*` routes continue to use `VertexGeminiAdapter`.
375	
376	Why:
377	Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite (`gemini-3.1-flash-lite`) returned `NOT_FOUND` through the configured Vertex project/region, but worked through the Gemini API using the deployed service-account OAuth path. The extraction A/B/C eval route `google_gemini_3_1_flash_lite_extraction_eval` depends on this split.
378	
379	Future sessions should:
380	Do not map `google/*` back to `vertex` in `packages/ai/src/routes/resolve.ts` unless the exact model has been verified in the configured Vertex region. `GEMINI_API_KEY` is optional; `GoogleGeminiAdapter` can also mint Gemini API OAuth tokens from `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON`.
381	
382	### Trigger.dev schedule slots are currently full
383	
384	What changed:
385	The `extraction-ab-eval` worker is an immediate Trigger.dev task with no cron sweep. A cron fallback was attempted on 2026-06-16, but Trigger.dev deploy failed because the project already had 10/10 schedules.
386	
387	Why:
388	Adding another `schedules.task()` currently blocks worker deployment. The A/B eval page queues rows and dispatches `extraction-ab-eval` immediately from Vercel through `TRIGGER_SECRET_KEY`.
389	
390	Future sessions should:
391	Do not add new Trigger schedules casually. Reuse/consolidate an existing schedule or increase the Trigger.dev schedule limit before adding another `schedules.task()`. Deploy workers with `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy`.
392	
393	### Teams transcript ids + the getAllTranscripts PULL endpoint (don't reintroduce these bugs)
394	
395	What changed:
396	Two non-obvious things in the scheduled-meeting transcript path (2026-06-24), both found by hitting them in prod:
397	1. `teams-transcript-ingestion` hashes a long derived transcript id to 32 chars for `client_message_id` (`deriveTranscriptId`). Scheduled online-meeting transcript ids are ~230-char base64 blobs; `teams:<id>:<n>` overflows `messages.client_message_id varchar(255)` and every insert fails. The full id is still kept in `metadata_json.transcriptId`.
398	2. The PULL endpoint for listing a user's transcripts is `users/{id}/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(...)` — NOT `communications/onlineMeetings/getAllTranscripts(...)`. The `communications/...` form is the tenant-wide **subscription** resource only; using it for a GET silently 404s (and a swallowed 404 looks like "no transcripts", so the scan returns 0 with no error).
399	
400	Why:
401	Both look like harmless simplifications but break ingestion/discovery for scheduled meetings specifically (ad-hoc "Meet Now" ids are short, so neither bug showed before).
402	
403	Future sessions should:
404	Keep the id hashing (don't revert to the raw id) and keep the two endpoints distinct (subscription resource vs per-user pull). `getOnlineMeetingTranscripts` is also deliberately resilient — a first-page 403 throws (real access gap), but a request timeout or Graph's `startIndex=-1` nextLink 400 keeps partial results; don't make it throw on every non-404.
405	
406	### Supabase project cutovers have platform integration surfaces
407	
408	What changed:
409	The production Supabase project moved from Ohio (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`, now `oracle.old`) to N. Virginia (`eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg`, `theoracle`) on 2026-06-20.
410	
411	Why:
412	A Supabase project cutover is not just `DATABASE_URL` / `DIRECT_URL` / browser key rotation. Vercel, GitHub, Trigger.dev, Supabase Auth providers, Microsoft Entra redirect URIs/client secrets, Supabase.com project integrations, and Recall live-bot envs can all hold project-specific URLs or secrets.
413	
414	Future sessions should:
415	Use `docs/deployment.md` "Supabase project cutover checklist" before declaring a cutover done. Vercel's Supabase integration may inject `SUPABASE_*` / `POSTGRES_*` env vars that this app does not read; the runtime still depends on `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_*`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, and `DIRECT_URL`.
416	
417	### The prod DIRECT host is IPv6-only — query prod via the session pooler from a local script
418	
419	Looks like:
420	The 1Password "Supabase DB Direct URL - The Oracle (CURRENT PROD …)" `password` field (`postgres@db.eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg.supabase.co:5432`) is the obvious connection string to run a local Node/psql script against prod.
421	
422	Actually:
423	That direct host now resolves to an **IPv6-only** address. A v4-only machine fails with `getaddrinfo ENOENT` (it resolved once mid-session, then stopped — don't trust intermittent success). Use the **session pooler** instead — same 1Password item, field **`oracle_session_pooler`**: `postgresql://postgres.eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg:<db-pw>@aws-1-us-east-1.pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres`.
424	
425	Why:
426	Username MUST be `postgres.<ref>` (plain `postgres` → `tenant/user not found`); the pool host prefix is `aws-1` not `aws-0` (Ohio was `aws-1-us-east-2`); the host is region-wide and the username's ref does the tenant routing. Don't guess the pool host — copy it from the Supabase dashboard **Connect → Session pooler**, or read the `oracle_session_pooler` field.

432	`DATABASE_URL`, and browser Supabase URL at the previous Ohio Supabase project
433	`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp` (`oracle.old`), not current prod

438	`DIRECT_URL`/`DATABASE_URL` with the current-prod 1Password item
439	`Supabase DB Direct URL - The Oracle (CURRENT PROD, theoracle,

443	### Diagram / flowchart image ingestion: the verbatim-quote vs relationship-claim tension
444	
445	What changed (2026-06-25, worker `20260625.1`):
446	`document-ingestion` now chunks structure-aware (`chunkTextStructured`), gives images a 32k extraction window, and switches to a handoff-oriented prompt when the transcription looks like a diagram (`looksLikeDiagramTranscription`). This made extraction emit `dependency`/handoff claims instead of one shallow `process_rule` per box.
447	
448	Actually (the trap):
449	A handoff claim ("After X, Y happens in dept Z") synthesizes an edge, but every claim's `exactQuote` must be a verbatim substring of ONE persisted chunk. It only validates if the vision transcription renders each edge as **one self-contained line** (`[A:"…"] --(Arrow:"cond")--> [B:"…"]`) AND the extractor quotes that whole line. When the vision pass instead splits an edge across two lines, the extractor paraphrases, the quote fails, and the claim is rejected — a re-eval of the test flowchart went 102 claims → **0** this way.
450	
451	Why this is fragile:
452	The vision model is non-deterministic and prod's `default_vision_route` was set to `google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` — an image-**generation** model, wrong for dense reading. One run produced a clean 13k-char single-line-edge transcription (claims validated); the next produced a truncated 2.5k-char split-line transcription (all claims rejected).
453	
454	Future sessions should:
455	UPDATE 2026-06-25: prod vision is now `qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking` and reads diagrams well (the earlier failure was an image-GENERATION model wrongly selected for reading + a silent fallback masking it). Use any real *vision* model (never an image-generation model). The current blocker has moved DOWNSTREAM to the extraction model (see the §14 row "EXTRACTION currently yields 0 claims"). If changing transcription/extraction prompts, keep one-line-per-edge in `IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM`. See HANDOFF "SESSION-END STATE" and the `scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` re-evaluation tool.
456	
457	### Model routing is fail-loud and pool-bounded
458	
459	Looks like:
460	The admin-selected model in Admin → Settings is the one that runs. The `model_runs` row showing a different provider/model looks like the configured choice.
461	
462	Actually:
463	Pipeline stages run through `resolveRouteCandidates(db, slot)`: the selected primary plus the approved DB pool, in order. Auxiliary slots (`vision`, `general`, `translation`) are explicit single-pick settings. If a model cannot dispatch, the router tries only the next approved candidate; if all candidates fail or a slot is unset, the call fails loud.
464	
465	Why:
466	The old hidden fallback path could run an unapproved model and still report success. The approved pool is the only acceptable fallback chain because admins can inspect and edit it.
467	
468	Do not change because:
469	Do not reintroduce route-level fallback IDs or worker hard-coded route constants. When debugging "wrong model ran," check `model_run_attempts` and the admin banner for failed primary or non-primary-success attempts, and confirm the provider's key/adapter is registered (`buildStandardAdapters` logs provider availability in prod).
470	
471	### Explicit Vertex caches are tracked in Postgres
472	
473	Looks like:
474	The adapter could rely only on in-memory cache handles.
475	
476	Actually:
477	`provider_cached_content` is the cross-process source of truth for explicit Vertex cache lifecycle, and `provider_metadata_json` now also tracks cleanup metadata for temporary GCS-backed cache sources.
478	
479	Why:
480	Workers and web requests run in different processes and time windows. Cache accounting and cleanup have to survive process boundaries.
481	
482	Do not change because:
483	Process-local cache tracking leaks money and makes cache reuse/cleanup invisible.
484	
485	### Qwen chat cache state is persisted separately from run usage
486	
487	Looks like:
488	`provider_response_sessions` duplicates data already in `model_runs`.
489	
490	Actually:
491	`model_runs` is audit history; `provider_response_sessions` stores the latest reusable `previous_response_id` keyed by logical session.
492	
493	Why:
494	Responses API session cache needs a stable conversation handle across requests and processes.
495	
496	Do not change because:
497	Without it, Qwen session cache resets every turn and the savings disappear.
498	
499	### Batch API methods on the adapter contract are optional
500	
501	Looks like:
502	`OracleProviderAdapter.submitBatch` and `retrieveBatch` are marked `?` (optional). Existing adapters (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen) don't implement them. The interface looks half-finished.
503	
504	Actually:
505	The methods are optional on purpose. DeepSeek has no public Batch API. Qwen's batch surface is non-OpenAI-compatible (DECISIONS.md D12 deferred the native DashScope SDK swap). OpenAI, Vertex, and Anthropic implement both methods today; future adapters opt in by implementing both.
506	
507	Why:
508	Forcing every adapter to implement batch would either block adding new providers behind a 50%-discount feature, or paper over it with stub `submitBatch` methods that throw — both worse than the optional pattern. The runtime helper `supportsBatch(adapter)` is the feature-detection contract. See DECISIONS.md D14.
509	
510	Do not change because:
511	Making `submitBatch` / `retrieveBatch` required on the interface would force the DeepSeek and Qwen adapters to throw at construction, which would break the per-provider `tryAdd()` boot in `buildStandardAdapters()` and silently disable both providers.
512	
513	Anthropic batch specifics: each request is a `messages.batches.create` entry with `custom_id` + `params` (same shape as a sync `messages.create`); when `jsonSchema` is provided we attach the forced single-tool input_schema per-request (mirrors `generateObject`). Status maps `processing_status: ended` → `'completed'` and we stream `messages.batches.results(id)` to produce `BatchResultItem`s. Per-item `result.type` → success (`succeeded` — tool_use input or text), or failure (`errored` / `canceled` / `expired`). `providerMetadata` is `{}` — the batch ID alone is sufficient for retrieve.
514	
515	### Two-phase batch worker — submit and drain are independent tasks
516	
517	Looks like:
518	`claim-extraction.ts`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts`, and `claim-extraction-batch-drain.ts` look redundant. The flag `extraction_dispatch_mode` toggles them, but all three are scheduled.
519	
520	Actually:
521	Each task reads `settings.extraction_dispatch_mode` at the top and bails when it doesn't match its dispatch mode. So at any given time only ONE of (sync) or (submit + drain) is active. The drain task is always scheduled because it always needs to poll outstanding `provider_batch_jobs` rows even after the flag flips back to `'sync'` (any in-flight batches should still be drained, not orphaned).
522	
523	Why:
524	The flag is read every cron tick — flipping it doesn't require a redeploy. The drain task running unconditionally ensures no orphaned batches when admin flips back to sync. Per-task short-circuit at the top is the cleanest gate.
525	
526	Do not change because:
527	Removing the always-on drain task would orphan any in-flight Vertex/OpenAI batches if the flag flipped back to sync mid-stream. Removing the gate from the sync task would double-process in batch mode.
528	
529	### Model catalog quality filter runs at BOTH write and read time
530	
531	Looks like:
532	The same filter logic appears in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts` (`refreshModelCatalog`) AND `apps/web/app/api/admin/model-catalog/route.ts` (`passesQualityFilter`). Looks duplicated.
533	
534	Actually:
535	Intentional defense in depth. The write-time filter prevents new junk from landing in the DB. The read-time filter handles the legacy case: rows written BEFORE the write-time filter shipped (or when OpenRouter enrichment was unavailable) sit in the DB with no pricing and no capability flags. Deleting them would break pool selections that still reference deprecated model IDs. Filtering at read time hides them from admins without losing the FK target.
536	
537	Why:
538	The write-time-only filter let junk creep back into the admin UI whenever an old OpenRouter outage produced unenriched rows. The read-time filter is the catch-all.
539	
540	Do not change because:
541	Removing the read-time filter resurrects the original "junky models reappear" bug. Removing the write-time filter floods the DB with no-data rows that build up over time. Keep both.
542	
543	### Design file operations are not product/design workflow
544	
545	Looks like:
546	Design-file naming, server organization, invalid filename characters, Photoshop/Illustrator file bloat, linked assets, packaging, and archive cleanup could live under `creative_design`, `product_development`, or `it_systems` because the design team and design tools are involved.

158	The blocking finding (the durable lesson):
159	- The new pipeline DID produce the right SHAPE of claims — re-ingest run `run_cmqsufh7644370uohnjafaayf` staged 15 `dependency` handoff claims (vs the old 79 one-box `process_rule`). **But all 15 failed `quote_exact_match`** → 0 promoted, so the doc went from 102 claims to **0**.
160	- Two causes: (1) the **vision pass is non-deterministic and was worse this run** — the transcription was 2,513 chars / 1 chunk and truncated mid-sentence (vs 12,928 chars / 4 chunks the first time), and used a format that splits each edge across two lines (node on one line, `--(Arrow)--> [target]` on the next), so no single verbatim line captures a handoff and the extractor paraphrased. (2) The first (good) run used **one self-contained line per edge**, which is exactly why its 16 dependency claims passed validation.
161	- **Likely root cause:** `settings.default_vision_route` in prod is `google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` — an image-**generation** preview model, the wrong tool for dense visual *reading*. The shipped fallback `vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary` (Gemini 2.5 Flash) is a real vision model and should be more consistent.

422	- **Images (vision)** — PNG/JPEG/WebP/HEIC. Two-pass: Pass 1 = a vision model transcribes the image to faithful text; Pass 2 = that text flows through the SAME chunk → extract → quote-validate → promote pipeline. The transcription is persisted as `document_chunks`, so the quote-validation provenance guarantee holds (claims quote the transcription, never the raw image). GIF/BMP/TIFF → unsupported.
423	- Pass 1 prompt (`IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM`) instructs a **structured text-topology** output for diagrams/flowcharts/org charts — nodes `[Shape/Color: "label"]`, edges `[A] --(condition)--> [B]`, swimlane `### headers` — with verbatim labels kept inside the nodes so they stay exactly quotable.

The file-backed Vertex cache path is skipped for images (a lone image is below the cache token minimum).
